DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY AS THEY IN PART ARISE OUT OF NEGLIGENCE (A DEMURRER CANNOT BE SUSTAINED ONLY TO A PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH POTENTIALLY ARISES OUT OF INTENTIONAL TORT). DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION INCLUDING A DUTY OWED TO THE SELLERS, BREACH OF THE DUTY OWED TO THE SELLERS AND DAMAGE SUSTAINED DUE TO THE BREACH OF DUTY OWED TO THE SELLERS. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF TO STATE A VIABLE CLAIM FOR ACTUAL CONTROVERSY THAT IS NOT OTHERWISE ADDRESSED IN THIS ACTION. IT APPEARS FROM THE OPPOSITION THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SEEKING A DETERMINATION REGARDING THE FEES AND COSTS THAT WILL BE INCURRED IN THIS ACTION, BUT DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION SUCH DETERMINATION IS A PROPER SUBJECT OF A SEPARATE CAUSE OF ACTION AS OPPOSED TO THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF/PART OF INDEMNITY CLAIM, ETC. Off Calendar Nov-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Pamela Albanese; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 213-250-1800; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendants Compass California II Inc. and Isabelle Grotte.
Loan Dao; Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP; 415-217-6990; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc.
Christopher W. Gribble; Gribble Legal Counsel; 510-365-5489; appearing as counsel for Defendants and Cross-Complainants John L. Yi and Patience Yi.
Randall F. Koenig; Koenig Law Firm; 949-756-0700; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Levy Art & Architecture, Inc.

Ms. Albanese contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS COMPASS CALIFORNIA II INC. AND ISABELLE GROTTE'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Reading the complaint as a whole, sufficient allegations are made against the sellers' agent/broker to state each cause of action irrespective of the indemnity clause (due to independent allegations regarding failure to discharge a duty, not the seller's own failures, which are alleged to have occurred as a result of reliance on the agent's counsel. See paragraph 53). The applicability of the doctrine of unclean hands raises factual disputes that cannot be addressed on a demurrer based on the SAC as alleged.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Pamela Albanese; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 213-250-1800; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendants Compass California II Inc. and Isabelle Grotte.
Loan Dao; Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP; 415-217-6990; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc.
Christopher W. Gribble; Gribble Legal Counsel; 510-365-5489; appearing as counsel for Defendants and Cross-Complainants John L. Yi and Patience Yi.
Randall F. Koenig; Koenig Law Firm; 949-756-0700; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Levy Art & Architecture, Inc.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part, DENIED in part, and moot in part. Motion is moot as to the allegations regarding duty in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Motion is granted as to the prayer for attorney's fees without prejudice for the Cross-Complainant to seek leave to amend if and when cross-complaint may make factual allegations to support such claim. The Court notes that cross-complainant failed to cite a single case in opposition standing for the proposition that joint tortfeasors, including intentional torts, may recover fees from each other. Motion is otherwise denied.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Pamela Albanese; Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP; 213-250-1800; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendants Compass California II Inc. and Isabelle Grotte.
Loan Dao; Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP; 415-217-6990; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc.
Christopher W. Gribble; Gribble Legal Counsel; 510-365-5489; appearing as counsel for Defendants and Cross-Complainants John L. Yi and Patience Yi.
Randall F. Koenig; Koenig Law Firm; 949-756-0700; appearing as counsel for Cross-Defendant Levy Art & Architecture, Inc.

Ms. Dao contests the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Gribble request that should the court adopt its tentative ruling, that cross-complainant be granted 30 days leave to amend. There being no objection, the court grants Mr. Gribbles request. The parties agree to wait until at least December 8, 2023 to submit a proposed order, and the court reminds the parties that the time for cross-complainant to amend is tolled from notice of entry of order. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for indemnity as they in part arise out of negligence (a demurrer cannot be sustained only to a part of a cause of action, which potentially arises out of intentional tort).
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence for the Cross-Complainant to allege all elements of this cause of action including a duty owed to the sellers, breach of the duty owed to the sellers and damage sustained due to the breach of duty owed to the sellers.
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief to state a viable claim for actual controversy that is not otherwise addressed in this action. It appears from the opposition that the cross-complaint is seeking a determination regarding the fees and costs that will be incurred in this action, but does not cite any authority for the proposition such determination is a proper subject of a separate cause of action as opposed to the prayer for relief/part of indemnity claim, etc. Cross-complainant shall have 30 days leave from notice of entry of order to amend.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
